response:
ISA, assume Walmart.com was developing a cross-border trade e-commerce marketplace integration platform. The platform aimed to streamline the process of online selling by small merchants in one country and online buying of those products across international borders by international shoppers, integrating Walmart E-commerce USA’s marketplace (walmart.com) with multiple of its Walmart International Online Marketplaces (e.g., Walmart U.K. (walmart.uk), Walmart Canada (walmart.ca), Walmart Mexico (walmart.mx)) and also with the international marketplaces of Walmart’s Strategic Partners like JD.com (China) and Rakuten.com (Japan). Top-tier U.K. companies already on the platform include STATSports, which has grown from its home base in Northern Ireland to be a global leader in wearable tech for professional and amateur sportspeople; Glasgow-based BuyBox, which sells home and garden products; Nodor, which manufactures and sells Red Dragon darts and other sporting equipment from its headquarters in Bridgend, Wales; and Leicester-based Pertemba, which exports a wide array of products to 18 countries worldwide.  

**Cross-Border Trade CBT E-commerce Marketplace Integration Platform Overview**:  
- *Purpose*: To develop a unified e-commerce platform that enables efficient B2B2C cross-border trade.  
- *Target Users*: Sellers and buyers in various international markets, including small businesses and large enterprises.  
- *Key Goals*: Enhance user experience, improve operational efficiency, and ensure compliance with international trade laws.  
The platform also includes Cross-border Fulfillment Services. Walmart Fulfillment Services (WFS) offers end-to-end fulfillment services for Walmart Marketplace sellers. With one of the world’s largest supply chains, Walmart is now building on it to provide the scale, quality, cost, and efficiency cross-border online marketplace sellers need to grow a successful business on any of Walmart’s global marketplaces.  

**Fulfillment Model 1: Ship with Walmart Cross-Border Trade E-Commerce Marketplace (Export Hub) Model**:  
Walmart Cross-Border Trade E-Commerce Marketplace Integration Planform is partnering with FedEx to provide this Logistics solution to the Sellers. In this model, FedEx would handle the customs duty and complete the last-mile delivery. The cross-border e-commerce marketplace sellers can use this service at a competitive cost. Sellers can use their own domestic shipping or FedEx as the carrier to ship to the FedEx Cross Border fulfillment center. However, their entire catalog must be shipped through only one (not multiple) Export Hub location.
